name: designing-components
description: Use this skill when you need to design a component
qualityReason: Generic, overlaps with other design skills
capabilities: Creates separate CSS files for component styling
Applies Tailwind CSS @apply directives for consistent styling
Organizes styles using @layer components for proper cascade
Follows project conventions for component file placement
author: Emz1998
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly identified Tailwind CSS directives (@apply, @layer, @utility, @variant) as shell backticks, and misinterpreted YAML frontmatter as weak cryptographic patterns. The skill is a legitimate component design guide containing only documentation and CSS styling instructions.
valueStatement: Developers need consistent, maintainable UI components but lack guidance on project conventions. This skill provides clear rules for designing components using Tailwind CSS, including file organization, styling patterns, and project structure.
limitations: Does not generate full component code including TypeScript or React
Focuses on Tailwind CSS, not other styling approaches

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers building new UI elements
useCases.title: Create a New UI Component
useCases.description: Design a new button, card, or form component following project conventions with proper Tailwind CSS organization.

useCases.targetUser: Developers maintaining or modernizing existing codebases
useCases.title: Refactor Existing Components
useCases.description: Update legacy components to use modern Tailwind CSS patterns with @layer components and @utility directives.

useCases.targetUser: Tech leads and architects setting design standards
useCases.title: Establish Component Standards
useCases.description: Define team-wide component design patterns using this skill as a reference for consistent styling architecture.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Component Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a component for [component-name] in this project. Follow the Tailwind CSS rules for creating components.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to create a simple component from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Component with Custom Styling
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a [component-name] component. It should use @utility directives for custom utilities and @variant directives for responsive states.
promptTemplates.scenario: When the component needs custom utilities or variants

promptTemplates.title: Nested Component Structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a [parent-component] that contains [child-component] elements. Use nested classes in @layer components for parent-child styling relationships.
promptTemplates.scenario: When designing components with child elements that need coordinated styling

promptTemplates.title: Full Component Package
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete [component-name] component including: separate CSS file, @layer components for styling, @utility directives for custom utilities, @variant directives for interactive states, and proper file placement in src/components.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a comprehensive component design following all project conventions

bestPractices: Use separate CSS files for each component to maintain separation of concerns and enable better caching
Organize styles within @layer components to ensure proper cascade order and prevent conflicts
Reference Tailwind v4 documentation for context-specific patterns before implementing custom solutions
antiPatterns: Avoid mixing inline styles with Tailwind utility classes as it breaks the utility-first pattern
Do not modify files in src/components/ui as these are reserved for shared UI library components
Avoid creating monolithic CSS files that combine multiple unrelated component styles

faq.question: What is the purpose of @layer components in Tailwind CSS?
faq.answer: The @layer components directive tells Tailwind to add styles to the components layer. This ensures proper cascade order, with base styles at the bottom, components in the middle, and utilities on top.

faq.question: How do @utility and @variant directives differ?
faq.answer: @utility creates custom utility classes like custom spacing or sizing. @variant creates custom state variants like hover:, focus:, or dark: for existing utilities.

faq.question: Where should new components be placed?
faq.answer: Create new components in the src/components root directory. Never modify files in src/components/ui as these are reserved for shared UI library components.

faq.question: Should I use nested classes for child elements?
faq.answer: Yes, use nested classes within @layer components for styling parent and child elements together. This keeps related styles co-located and easier to maintain.

faq.question: What Tailwind v4 documentation should I reference?
faq.answer: Refer to the context files for theme variables, custom styles, functions and directives, detecting classes in source files, and the upgrade guide for Tailwind v4 specific patterns.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with CSS frameworks other than Tailwind?
faq.answer: No, this skill is specifically designed for Tailwind CSS. The @apply, @layer, @utility, and @variant directives are Tailwind-specific features.
